# Speed Matrix results - full run 2026-07-18

Run: https://github.com/interactivetools-com/SmartString/actions/runs/29670857386
(25 cells: PHP 8.1-8.5 x linux-x64/linux-arm/windows-x64/macos-x64/macos-arm,
scale=1.0, JIT off). Generated by speed-probe.php per cell, merged by
speed-merge.php; see speed-probe.php for what each test measures.

To regenerate: dispatch the Speed Matrix workflow (Actions tab or
`gh workflow run speed-matrix.yml`), download the probe-* artifacts, then
`php speed-merge.php probes/*.json`.

Verdicts from this run (details in the per-test rows below):

- **gate-preg** (identity fast path): ADOPTED in SmartString 3.0 - see
  ENCODE_SKIP_REGEX in src/SmartString.php. Positive on all 25 cells.
- **arr-get** (folded __get): adopt in SmartArray; union return kept
  (arr-get-mixed gain does not reproduce cross-platform).
- **prop-type**: small consistent gain, optional.
- **promo**, **memo-mix**: ties everywhere - settled, not worth adopting.
- **gate-adaptive** (strspn on 8.4+): rejected - loses on short strings on
  every platform, including 8.4+.
- **strspn-cliff**: documents why - strspn is ~50x slower than preg scanning
  before PHP 8.4 on every platform.
- **tier-strrep**, **gate-unicode**: deferred phase-2 candidates
  (tier-strrep is platform-skewed: big on Windows/Linux, ~1.0x on macOS).
- **no-object**: ceiling reference only (~2x), parked.
- **idiom**: ->htmlEncode() is a few percent faster than (string) cast -
  docs tip, not a code change.

## Speed matrix: B-vs-A ratios (>1.00 = candidate faster)

Correctness: every encoder byte-identical on every cell.

<details><summary>Test legend (A vs B)</summary>

- **promo**: manual assignment vs ctor promotion
- **prop-type**: typed readonly prop vs untyped prop (typed ctor)
- **gate-preg-short**: htmlspecialchars vs preg identity gate
- **gate-preg-1kb**: htmlspecialchars vs preg identity gate
- **gate-preg-mix**: htmlspecialchars vs preg identity gate
- **gate-miss-short**: htmlspecialchars vs gate (always misses)
- **gate-miss-1kb**: htmlspecialchars vs gate (always misses)
- **strspn-cliff**: preg gate scan vs strspn scan
- **gate-adaptive-short**: preg-only gate vs version-adaptive gate
- **gate-adaptive-1kb**: preg-only gate vs version-adaptive gate
- **tier-strrep**: htmlspecialchars vs gated str_replace tier
- **gate-unicode**: two-tier (bails on UTF-8) vs three-tier (/u pass)
- **arr-get**: current 3-call chain vs folded single-lookup __get
- **arr-get-mixed**: tuned, union return vs tuned, mixed return
- **no-object**: object + __toString vs direct encoded string
- **idiom**: (string) cast vs ->htmlEncode()
- **memo-mix**: gate only vs gate + memo cache

</details>
